title: His Name Shall Be Called Wonderful
summary: “For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given: and the government shall be upon his shoulder: and his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace.” Isaiah 9:6 The Hebrew word for “wonderful” is pele' and means “a miracle, marvelous thing, a wonder. Indeed, the Lord Jesus Christ is a miracle. A miracle can be defined as a welcome, unexpected event that cannot be explained by natural laws but a divine act. In his birth, the Lord Jesus was wonderful; angels, shepherds and wise men from the east came to marvel at his miraculous birth of a virgin. “Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign; Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel” (Isaiah 7:14). As a 12-year-old, the doctors of the law marveled at the wisdom Jesus demonstrated in the seat of theological learning, the Temple in Jerusalem. The three-year ministry of the Lord was filled with non-stop gasps, shouts and screams of relief and joy on account of healings and deliverances wherever Jesus went. An eyewitness of the ministry of the Lord, John, wrote, “And there are also many other things which Jesus did, the which, if they should be written everyone, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written. Amen” (John 21:25). His death and resurrection were also equally wonderful.
